growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: patric.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: patric.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Brackish carbonate-buffered sulfate-reducer medium (Widdel & Bak 1992, without sulfate) + North Sea crude oil

up.method: alkane removal quantified (stoichiometric with methane) but per-biomass specific uptake rate not reported

sec.compound: acetate + H2 (syntrophic alkane oxidation to acetate and hydrogen)
sec.method: pathway confirmed (Table 2 Eq.4) but specific secretion rate not quantified per organism

yields.note: assuming Smithella responsible for all alkane degradation

growth_records.cite: Gray et al. The quantitative significance of Syntrophaceae and syntrophic partnerships in methanogenic degradation of crude oil alkanes. Environmental Microbiology (2011). DOI:10.1111/j.1462-2920.2011.02570.x. [PMC3258425]
growth_records.snippet: The specific growth rate (mu) was 0.019 day-1 corresponding to a doubling time of 36 days and the growth yield was 0.020 g(cell-C)/g(alkane-C) assuming that it was responsible for all alkane degradation.
growth_records.notes: Environmental microcosm (mixed community), not pure culture. mu reported 0.019 day-1 from exponential fit (N_T=N_0*e^(mu*t), R2=0.9) of qPCR 16S gene abundance over 0-450 d; converted mu = 0.019/24 = 7.9e-4 /h. Doubling time reported 36 days = 36*24 = 864 h. Growth yield 0.020 g cell-C/g alkane-C reported directly. Incubation temperature/pH not stated in this paper (microcosm prep cited to Jones et al 2008). Alkane uptake and acetate/H2 secretion documented qualitatively but not as specific per-biomass rates.
